key_insight: QOC-Workbench uses an LLM to autonomously generate, test, and transfer quantum optimal control strategies across different hardware and Hamiltonian settings, enabling cross-paradigm protocol design beyond fixed-form numerical tuning.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to design hardware-compliant quantum control protocols for annealing, state engineering, and adiabatic computation with less expert intervention.
method: LLM parses physics literature and proposes structural control hypotheses
LLM writes simulation code to validate candidate protocols
Learns reusable control motifs and, for harder cases, shifts to an amortized GNN generator for scalable coefficient paths
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful demonstrations in three settings—Rydberg MIS arrays, XXZ spin chains, and random transverse-field Ising models—with protocols that outperform literature baselines and transfer to larger unseen systems.
watch_item: Because the workflow depends on LLM-generated hypotheses and simulation-based validation, reliability and reproducibility will hinge on rigorous auditing, benchmark coverage, and hardware-accurate modeling.
paper_title: LLM-Driven Cross-Paradigm Design for Quantum Optimal Control
